title: Dactylorchis lapponica
taxonomy: Dactylorchis lapponica is a species of orchid. It is classified within the family Orchidaceae. More specifically, it is a member of the subtribe Orchidinae. Within this subtribe, it is classified in the genus Dactylorchis. The classification of Dactylorchis lapponica is further detailed in the botanical nomenclature.
uses: Dactylorchis lapponica is a species of orchid native to the Arctic regions of Europe, Asia, and North America. intro: Dactylorchis lapponica, also known as Lapland orchid or Lady's-slipper orchid, is a type of terrestrial orchid native to the Arctic and alpine regions of Europe, Asia, and North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and treat respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Dactylorchis lapponica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Dactylorchis lapponica is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat colds, flu, and other respiratory ailments.
